prompt: A single-frame, dramatic, top-down photograph looking into a colossal, ancient, subterranean sinkhole that functions as a monumental data visualization of socioeconomic deciles. The overall structure is an immense, circular pit, built of cracked, rough stone and metal, set into ancient rock, creating an archaeological, industrial-archeological aesthetic. The concentric, descending tiers are explicitly marked I through X in ancient, stone-carved Roman numerals along the inner walls, delineating the 10 depth/decile levels. The topmost ring is a vast, ornate, golden-frieze rim, shining with warm, luminous golden light. This ring is entirely covered by intricately carved resource symbols, all clustered here to signal total hoarding: stylized high-rise skylines, train tracks, golden wheat stalks, and an ancient-looking stylized sunburst interwoven with elaborate data streams (non-digital filigree), which make its physical volume visibly disproportionately greater than any lower level. The physical depth of each numbered tier is non-uniform, precisely reflecting the actual data distribution. The wall segment directly above numeral I is exceptionally deep, visualizing the top decile's 50% share as a vast vertical chasm. Subsequent tiers I, II, III have deep, intermediate heights, while tiers IV through X (the bottom 60%) are visually compressed into an incredibly narrow, dense band of stone near the bottom of the pit, illustrating their minimal combined share. Along the stone walls of tiers I-X, there is a visible gradient countdown of small, eroded resource icons and fractures: Tier I has 10 tiny, cracked grain icons; Tier V has 5; Tier IX has 1; Tier X is just fractured rubble, visualizing total scarcity. A single, colossal, broken wooden and twine ladder descends from a fixed point on the gilded golden top frieze, dropping vertically through all the broken and grey rings, highlighting a visceral statistical cliff. The first massive drop from the golden rim to Tier I is a pure vertical gap with the ladder hanging freely from a pulley, its rungs visibly smashed, creating a failed ascent point. The ladder restarts at Tier I but has multiple broken rungs concentrated around Tier IV, drawing the eye to key statistical thresholds. At the very bottom of the compressed pit, huddling in the darkest rubble filled chasm void, are the tiny, solitary human figures, slightly more illuminated with a soft, weak, flickering light seeping from the broken middle levels, emphasizing their vulnerability against the colossal scale. The entire structure has an archeological and monumental aesthetic. Lighting is dramatic, moving from strong golden light at the top to dark, fractured stone below. The vertiginous perspective forces vertigo. The meaning is carried entirely by the visual hierarchy, condition, materials, and count of rings, without text, labels, standard charts, or legends present.
insight: The structural impossibility of economic mobility in a top-heavy economy: while the top decile hoards a vastly disproportionate share of India's economic growth and resources, the bottom majority shares a statistically negligible fraction, severed from any functional path to upward economic mobility
